campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's finances for July 2025, including how the city's investment accounts performed and overall spending. The council also discussed plans for improving transportation options around Campbell, looking at ways to make it easier for residents to get around using different methods beyond just cars.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment account performance and transactions
City budget and spending report for July
New multimodal transportation improvements (buses, biking, walking options)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The City Council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could share their views on a proposed new city rule. After hearing from the public, the council moved forward by skipping the usual first reading and planning to vote on adopting the ordinance at their next meeting.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input on new ordinance
Council waived first reading to expedite the process
Ordinance scheduled for final vote at next regular meeting
Specific ordinance topic not detailed in agenda summary

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from various March sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The agenda focused mainly on official record-keeping from previous meetings and moving forward with a construction project.
keyTopics: Approved multiple sets of meeting minutes from March 2026 sessions
Authorized construction contract with QLM, Inc. (details incomplete in provided agenda)
Town Manager given authority to execute the construction agreement

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council reviewed how well the city is serving kids and teens, including youth programs like the Safe Summer Initiative and mentorship services. The council also looked at how code enforcement is working in neighborhoods and checked on the status of library upgrades and improvements.
keyTopics: Youth programs and summer activities for kids and teens
Code enforcement performance across the city
Library facility improvements and customer service updates
Youth mentorship and intervention services

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, that was available both in-person and online. Residents who wanted to speak during the meeting could join via Zoom or phone and raise their hand when their topic came up.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for remote participants
Online participation instructions for residents

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026, where they honored the flag and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group also approved the official notes from their previous meeting in March.
keyTopics: Personal stories from three commissioners about living in Sunnyvale
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Flag salute ceremony

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met to discuss three main topics: a presentation about Budget and Bites (likely a food/nutrition program), a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and information about summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved meeting minutes from March and opened the floor for residents to speak about issues they care about.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth organization
City of Cupertino summer jobs opportunities
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, where residents could participate either in person or remotely via Zoom. The city is making it easier for people to stay involved in local government by offering multiple ways to attend and comment on issues being discussed.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom options available)
Remote participation via Zoom with meeting ID 997-0675-9306
Phone call-in option for those without internet access
Written public comments can be submitted through eComment system on City Council Agenda page

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met on April 7, 2026 to handle routine business and plan ahead. Main items included updating the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2026, reviewing requests from individual council members for new projects, and considering changes to how the city fills council vacancies.
keyTopics: Federal funding plan update (Consolidated Plan for 2025-2030)
Council members' requests for new workplan items
Changes to how council vacancies are filled
Approval of previous meeting minutes and scheduling

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council reviewed and approved the city's gas utility budget and pricing plans for the next fiscal year. The main decision involved approving new gas rate schedules for residential customers, businesses, and large commercial users, along with transferring funds between accounts to keep the utility financially stable.
keyTopics: Approval of fiscal year 2027 gas utility budget and financial forecast
Changes to residential gas rates (Schedule G-1)
Updates to commercial and large business gas rates (Schedules G-2 and G-3)
Reserve and general fund transfers for the gas utility
Review of previous Utilities Advisory Commission meeting minutes from March 4
